title: 10 medicinal constituents of Alchemilla vulcanica (lady's mantle)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Alchemilla vulcanica flavonoids is a group of plant compounds that are responsible for its medicinal properties. These flavonoids have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to the herb's traditional uses in treating digestive issues, wound healing, and menstrual disorders. Additionally, they may help protect against cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer.

Alchemilla vulcanica gallic acid is a type of phenolic compound found in the plant. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, wounds, and menstrual disorders. Gallic acid exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the biological activities and potential therapeutic applications of this compound.

Alchemilla vulcanica quercetin is a flavonoid compound extracted from its leaves and flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and skin conditions. Quercetin exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease, cancer, and cognitive decline. Additionally, it shows potential for modulating the immune system and reducing the risk of infections.

Alchemilla vulcanica kaempferol is a flavonoid compound found in its leaves and flowers. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Kaempferol has been shown to exhibit potent antimicrobial effects against various microorganisms, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing and infection prevention.

Alchemilla vulcanica ellagic acid is a potent antioxidant compound extracted from its leaves and flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, wound healing, and cancer prevention. Ellagic acid has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, making it a promising natural remedy for diverse diseases. Its bioactive properties also contribute to its potential as an anti-aging agent.

Alchemilla vulcanica ursolic acid is a triterpenoid compound found in its leaves. This compound has been studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Ursolic acid has been shown to have inhibitory effects on the growth of certain cancer cell lines, making it a promising area of research for the development of new cancer treatments.

Alchemilla vulcanica betulinic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from the plant. It has been found to possess remarkable antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for various diseases. Studies have shown that betulinic acid exhibits strong cytotoxic effects against cancer cells, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Its unique chemical structure makes it an attractive target for further research and development of novel pharmaceuticals.

Alchemilla vulcanica oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid compound extracted from the plant's leaves and stems. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammatory disorders, wounds, and skin conditions. Recent studies have confirmed its potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising natural remedy for modern health applications.

Alchemilla vulcanica salicylic acid is a key compound extracted from its leaves and stems. This naturally occurring phenolic acid has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various skin conditions, such as acne, eczema, and psoriasis. It possesses antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and combating microbial infections.

Alchemilla vulcanica caffeoylquinic acids is a type of polyphenolic compound found in the plant's leaves and stems. These compounds have been extensively studied for their potential therapeutic properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Caffeoylquinic acids have been shown to possess wound-healing properties, inhibit cancer cell growth, and exhibit neuroprotective effects, making them a promising area of research in the development of novel pharmaceuticals and dietary supplements.

intro: Alchemilla vulcanica has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. These compounds confer medicinal properties that have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and menstrual problems. The flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe gastrointestinal complaints and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Additionally, the triterpenoids have estrogenic properties, making lady's mantle a natural remedy for menopausal symptoms and uterine health issues.
intro_desc: Alchemilla vulcanica has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and astringent effects. The flavonoids can improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and lipid profiles, while the phenolic acids may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. These constituents can improve people's lives by enhancing overall well-being and reducing the risk of chronic diseases.
